payload.answer: Extracted architecture decisions: Brain DB is the source of truth; Cognee is a rebuildable semantic projection; Slack is a strict memory intake agent. Preserved open questions: whether to add Telegram later and which model is cheapest while saturating performance.
citations: Brain DB should be the source of truth.
Cognee should be a rebuildable semantic projection.
Slack should be a strict memory intake agent.
Open questions: Should Telegram be added later? Which model is cheapest while saturating performance?
payload.decision: Commit stable architecture decisions as memory; preserve open questions without treating them as decided.

entity_resolution.reason: No existing entity IDs or ambiguous duplicate entities were provided; extracted entities can be resolved downstream.

payload.intent: Extract durable architectural takeaways from the provided Brain Architecture chat summary.

memory_cards.statement: Brain DB should be the source of truth for the Brain memory system.
Cognee should be a rebuildable semantic projection rather than the source of truth.
Slack should be a strict memory intake agent for Brain.
It is undecided whether Telegram should be added later as a Brain integration.
It is undecided which model is cheapest while still saturating performance for Brain-related workloads.

details: Committed only stated architecture decisions as durable memory candidates.
Represented Telegram and model choice as open questions, not decisions.
No conflicts or superseded/deleted facts were present in the input.
